question: Open the window for me please, __________ you?
In an analytical essay, each paragraph should begin with a __________.
The young child was __________ by the loud thunder and hid under the bed.
The criminal __________ on the train when the police were informed by telegram.
My brother __________ a bear an hour ago while hiking in the forest.
A: 'Reem was wearing some beautiful shoes.' B: '__________ ? She always looks stylish.'
After moving abroad, they found it hard to __________ in touch with family.
Can you help me __________ why my phone keeps turning off randomly?
Jordan is hoping __________ use AI sensibly in the coming years.
Read: 'How could a young English woman with no scientific training possibly make contact with a group of wild chimpanzees?' — This sentence is:
She __________ three languages — Arabic, English and French.
She was completely __________ when she saw her name at the top of the results list.
We __________ never eaten Japanese food before — this is our first time.
A: 'It's my birthday tomorrow!' B: '__________ ? How are you going to celebrate?'
Read: 'some people are worried that students using AI might end up with incorrect work. AI search engines do not always use reliable sources.' — What is the concern about AI search engines?
choices: They do not always use reliable sources, so information may be incorrect
They are too expensive for schools
They are too slow for students

question: She __________ a letter to her pen friend when the electricity went out suddenly.
She __________ some Spanish words just by listening to her neighbours speak.
She __________ as being very confident when she gave her presentation.
She __________ to work by car, but this week she is using public transport.
By the time the meeting was over, the rain __________.
Read: 'But are we now forgetting how to communicate face-to-face?' — The author uses this question to:
choices: Express concern about over-reliance on technology
Prove that face-to-face communication is impossible

question: She tried to __________ her point, but the audience didn't seem to understand.
The interviewer asked me who was responsible — he really __________.
The ambassador was sent to __________ contact between the two governments.
She couldn't __________ what the sign said because the letters were too small.
Let's have a party to celebrate this good news, __________?
A: 'Jawad didn't want to play tennis.' B: '__________ ? Maybe he was tired.'
They __________ to each other regularly back then, but have lost touch now.
A: 'I've got to give a speech in class.' B: '__________ is the worst that could happen?'
It's a beautiful day for a walk, __________?
She __________ a text message right now, so she cannot talk to you.
I __________ him immediately — we had so much in common from the start.
A: 'I used to live in France.' B: '__________ ? I bet it's very beautiful.'
A: 'Majeda and Malak have arrived downstairs.' B: '__________ ? I'll come right away.'
Read: 'Since the dawn of time, people have been using different ways of communicating at a distance. In ancient times, they used smoke signals.' — What did people use in ancient times to communicate?
I said the topic was closed, but he __________ — he would not stop talking about it.
A: 'They've built a new hospital in our area.' B: '__________ ? That's great news.'
He didn't mean to be unfriendly, but he __________ as cold in the interview.
Read: 'Here she came across a scientist who asked Jane if she might want a job observing chimpanzees in the wild. She immediately said yes.' — Why did Jane go to Tanzania?
choices: She wanted to meet the Goodall family
She went to write a book about Africa
A scientist offered her a job observing chimpanzees in the wild

question: 'I __________ what you mean — please explain again.' — Choose the correct form.
